items.title: Drug charges laid after women allegedly try to evade police after exiting Spirit ferry

children.content: Two women are alleged to have had traffickable quantities of meth and cocaine in their car when they evaded police upon exiting the Spirit of Tasmania ferry in Devonport.

image.altText: A large quantity of illicit drugs and tobacco on a wooden table.

items.title: Half a billion litres of improperly treated sewage released into waterways

children.content: More than half a billion litres of improperly treated sewage were released into the state's waterways in the 2024 to 2025 financial year, including at popular swimming spots such as Launceston's Cataract Gorge.

image.altText: People lounge on the grass next to a natural body of water.

items.title: Government department apologises for accidentally underpaying staff

children.content: A Tasmanian government department will rectify the underpayment of more than 900 current and former staff members because it misunderstood the award.

gallery.altText: A man in a white shirt and tie.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a white shirt and tie.

items.title: Serpentine leafminer detected in Tasmania for first time

children.content: The insect has been found at a commercial grower in Tasmania's north-west. It damages the leaves of the plants it attacks and can reduce crop yield.

image.altText: A fly-like insect with wings sits on a green leaf.

items.title: Two dead in car crash in Tasmania's south

children.content: Two vehicles collided at Old Beach in Tasmania's south earlier today. The two occupants of one of the vehicles have died at the scene, while the other driver is uninjured.

image.altText: Several police cars and an ambulance seen on a stretch of road that turns a corner, with a river on one side.

items.title: 'Proud to come from this place': Devils captain Jye Menzie on his upbringing

children.content: Jye Menzie grew up in the north of Hobart, known for some of the nation's worst socio-economic outcomes and a high level of disadvantage. But the Tasmania Devils VFL captain is proud of where he comes from, and would not have it any other way.

image.altText: man black and green jacket

items.title: Poultry producers warn bird flu would be 'catastrophic for Tasmania'

children.content: Tasmanian poultry producers are ramping up biosecurity measures, as they prepare for the arrival of a deadly strain of bird flu. The state government has stressed that it's well prepared, but says euthanasia is "definitely possible" if there's an outbreak in commercial poultry.

image.altText: Brad stands in front of his farm gate, which has a biosecurity sign on it. He crosses his arms and looks at the camera.

items.title: Why Boag's being bought by another brewer is 'fantasy'

children.content: A union is calling on the brewery's owner to sell the business and brand, rather than close down production in Tasmania. But a local brewer explains why that's unlikely to happen.

gallery.altText: A red brick building with a large Boags Brewery sign.

thumbnail.altText: A red brick building with a large Boags Brewery sign.

items.title: Pauline Hanson says Socceroos represent her vision of monoculture

children.content: Hanson says she is "delighted" monoculturalism is being debated and says the Australian soccer team represents her vision for a national "monoculture". Follow live.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson and other One Nation senators in the senate.

items.title: 'I love you': Sydney shark attack survivor's first words to family

children.content: A Sydney mother mauled by a shark at Coogee Beach almost a fortnight ago has spoken her first words after briefly waking from an induced coma.

image.altText: Sydney mother and Coogee local, Leah Stewart, who was mauled by a shark holds a baby

images.altText: Congo DR fan Michel Nkuka Mboladinga stands in among the crowd
Daniel Munoz holds his hands on his head and reacts in disbelief
Harry Kane sinks to his knees and puts his head in his hands

articles.title: Quick hits: Trump's special job and commentator banned for outburst

children.content: Cristiano Ronaldo makes a bit of World Cup history as Gianni Infantino offers an update on the future of the hydration breaks and Donald Trump is given role at this year's final. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Donald Trump, Cristiano Ronaldo, Gianni Infantino

articles.title: 'Doesn't seem right': Why Socceroos won't play for a draw against Paraguay

children.content: A draw would be enough for the Socceroos to progress from their World Cup group, but according to defender Jason Geria, playing for anything less than a win does not sit right with this group.

image.altText: Jordan Bos #5 of Australia is tackled by Hakan Calhanoglu #10 of Turkiye

articles.title: England's hunt for attacking spark fruitless, as 'positivity' takes over

children.content: England say they need to stay positive after a dour goal-less draw against Ghana, positivity that is hopefully easier to find than shots on target were against the Black Stars' low block in Boston.

image.altText: Harry Kane holds his head

children.content: England is held to a frustrating 0-0 draw by a resolute Ghana after Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice in a 5-0 Portugal win over Uzbekistan. Follow live.

items.title: Chaotic start to Colombia clash, England frustrated by resolute Ghana

updates.title: FT: Colombia hold on to qualify for knockout stage

children.content: England say they need to stay positive after a dour goal-less draw against Ghana, positivity that is hopefully easier to find than shots on target were against the Black Stars' low block in Boston.

items.title: 'Historic blunder': US Senate votes to seek to prevent further military action in Iran

children.content: It is the 10th time the Senate has tried to stop the war, and the outcome, on a vote of 50–48, is a stunning turnaround from past efforts.

image.altText: A group of politicians surround a podium with hands raised.

items.title: He's been out for 50 years, but many Gardening Australia fans wouldn't know

children.content: Gardening Australia star Jerry Coleby-Williams hid his identity on TV for 26 years, but has never been ashamed of being a gay man.

gallery.altText: Jerry Coleby-Williams stands outside smiling, holding up a hanging pot overflowing with cherry tomatoes
A young Jerry Coleby-Williams stands next to a film camera and two cameramen in a garden

items.title: Experts ask why triple murderer was granted bail before deadly spree

children.content: Sophie Quinn sought police protection from her former partner. Less than two months later he murdered her and two others during a shooting spree at Lake Cargelligo.

image.altText: A sign of a police sign in blue, and a courthouse sign for Lake Cargelligo.

items.title: Limits for 'silent killer' chemical exposure on hold despite advice

children.content: A majority of state and territory governments have put stricter workplace exposure limits on hold for nine hazardous chemicals.

image.altText: A worker in hi-vis clothing walks across a sandy construction site with machinery and Fiona Stanley Hospital in the background.

items.title: Illegal e-bikes crushed after Tweed-Byron police crackdown

children.content: Police on the NSW Far North Coast have confiscated and crushed 35 e-bikes, and warn even more could soon be trashed as the crackdown continues.

image.altText: A video still of e-bikes being crushed at a scrap yard.

items.title: IVF add-ons are common in Australia, but there's no proof most work

children.content: Experts urge clinics and specialists to "carefully consider" offering IVF add-on treatments as they can be seen as "implicit endorsement of benefit" when there's little evidence most work.

image.altText: A composite image of a woman smiling with a needle and a woman taking a selfie.

items.title: Farming couple who met at bus stop celebrate 70 years of love on the land

children.content: Farming couple Bill and Val Collins share their secrets to a long marriage as they celebrate their platinum wedding anniversary.

image.altText: An elderly couple look lovingly at each other

items.title: When Siri Hustvedt felt her husband's ghost, she turned to science

children.content: The author of What I Loved has published a moving memoir drawing on letters and journals documenting the last days of Paul Auster, her partner in life and writing.

gallery.altText: An old photo of a young couple: a blonde woman in a singlet and shorts, and a dark-haired man in a short-sleeved shirt.

thumbnail.altText: An old photo of a young couple: a blonde woman in a singlet and shorts, and a dark-haired man in a short-sleeved shirt.

items.title: The 'uncontrolled' police department behind fatal shooting of Perth girl

children.content: The grandfather of Australian schoolgirl Hania Ahmed recounts the aftermath of her fatal shooting, as a senior Pakistani lawyer takes aim at the "uncontrolled" police department at the centre of her death.

Selfie of a man hugging his young daughter and forming finger signs.
Mazhar sitting down with a sad, troubled expression.

items.title: Port Moresby's water supply network showing 'signs of imminent failure'

children.content: A decades-old pipeline supplying water to nearly 1 million people in Papua New Guinea's capital is at risk of collapsing without urgent repairs, threatening to cut off more than half of the city's water supply.

image.altText: Water bursts from two points in a long concrete flume.

items.title: Construction company hit by alleged multi-million-dollar insider fraud

children.content: Queensland police have uncovered an alleged industrial espionage operation that caused major construction company Shadforth's confidential data to be leaked to competitors.

image.altText: ute in front of building

items.title: Ex-CEO vindicated in $2.3 million court ruling against space firm

children.content: Once a promising space start-up chief executive, Carley Scott was terminated from her job and left millions out of pocket. Now she's finally found vindication, with a court ruling in her favour.

image.altText: A woman with a brown ponytail wearing hi-vis smiles at a worksite

items.title: Australians financially supporting family in Lebanon say they have no other option

children.content: Lebanese Australians, who have spent thousands of dollars to help their loved ones survive a two-and-a-half-year war, bear a financial burden that also comes with an emotional toll.

image.altText: A photo of Sue (Far right) with her family pictured behind them in Lebanon.

items.title: UN inquiry accuses Israel of deliberately targeting and killing Palestinian children

children.content: The Israeli government is condemning the inquiry and rejecting its findings, labelling the United Nations report "deeply flawed" and full of "errors and distortions" designed to push a "politically driven anti-Israel narrative".

image.altText: A young child looking through a makeshift tent's window.

items.title: 'I get a lot of anxiety': Borrowers caught out with high-interest car loans

children.content: A new report warns customers about traps they face when taking out a car loan, including high-interest rates and fees.

gallery.altText: A woman with a leg injury sits in the front seat of her car holding the steering wheel.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with a leg injury sits in the front seat of her car holding the steering wheel.

items.title: Victorian taxi drivers face punishment for refusing short trips

children.content: The government is expanding its "two strikes and you're out" rules to include drivers who refuse to take passengers with assistance dogs or who knock back short trips.

image.altText: Yellow taxis in Melbourne.

items.title: Outgoing British PM holds meeting with likely successor

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer and the man who has essentially forced him to resign as prime minister, Andy Burnham, have held their first face-to-face meeting.

image.altText: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer looks sideways.

items.title: Australia launches world-first suicide prevention approach

children.content: Policymakers, mental health experts and those working on the front line will use the National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to gauge what services are working to prevent people from reaching critical crisis points.

gallery.altText: Jo Riley smiles while in a garden. She is wearing a colourful top and a necklace.

thumbnail.altText: Jo Riley smiles while in a garden. She is wearing a colourful top and a necklace.

items.title: 'Punched in the guts': Sacked academics take uni to Fair Work Commission

children.content: Two senior academics from Macquarie University allege they were targeted for redundancy because of their union activity.

image.altText: A middle aged woman with short white hair sitting in a loungeroom

items.title: Congregants say final goodbye at funeral for church

children.content: Anglican Bishop Keith Joseph is farewelling churches as his diocese pays its obligations to child abuse victims.

image.altText: A man in bishop's garments and a cross around his neck rests his hand on a pew, an empty church behind him

items.title: Five key takeaways from Crisafulli's second budget

children.content: This year's Queensland budget includes no "sugar hits" when it comes to cost of living, a big interest repayment bill and plenty of construction projects in the pipeline.

image.altText: David Janetzki and david crisafulli deliver a press conference about the budget

items.title: Budget offers short-term sugar hit but there could be bitterness to come

children.content: Faced with rising interest rates, high grocery prices and bloated bills, the cost-of-living help announced in Tuesday's budget will only make a minor dent in household budgets, Nick Dole writes.

image.altText: photo of a hand with empty cup of coffee and cash on a wooden table

avatar.alt: ABC reporter Nick Dole looking straight at the camera with a grey background

items.title: Asteroid crater in Pilbara confirmed again as world's oldest

children.content: Researchers find a crater in WA's East Pilbara is 3 billion years old, but it has sparked debate in the scientific community.
